summary: Large early data-labeling PR that replaced many raw .incbin ranges in asm/data.s and asm/sdata.s with explicit .asciz strings plus .balign 4 padding, preserving remaining unknown bytes as narrower .incbin chunks. The strings expose source filenames, assert expressions, debug/error messages, archive/file names, data-table names, and subsystem breadcrumbs across lb, camera, player, map collision, effects, fighter, and related code. The author briefly asked maintainers to hold off while dumping missed strings, then marked it ready; no substantive review feedback is present in the slice.
what_changed: Converted many anonymous raw data ranges in asm/data.s from broad .incbin directives into explicit .asciz strings followed by .balign 4.
Converted many anonymous raw data ranges in asm/sdata.s similarly, although the excerpt mostly shows asm/data.s.
Kept unknown or non-string bytes as adjusted smaller .incbin ranges after known strings, preserving offsets while exposing text.

Exposed file-name strings such as lbvector.c, lbshadow.c, lbspdisplay.c, lbmemory.c, lbheap.c, lbfile.c, lbarchive.c, lbcardnew.c, lbcardgame.c, lbsnap.c, lbmthp.c, camera.c, cmsnap.c, player.c, pltrick.c, plbonus.c, plbonuslib.c, mpcoll.c, mpisland.c, lbrefract.c, efasync.c, and fighter.c.

Exposed assert-condition strings including !(jobj->flags & JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION), !image_desc->image_ptr, !p->size, !cancelflag, p->status == LbHeapStatus_Create, entry_num != -1, free_index != -1, index < _p(slot)[chan].num, heap_size >= memoryRequired, fov_u<MTXDegToRad(90.0F), 0 <= h_player && h_player < 8, and item/player range checks.

Exposed runtime/debug/error strings including Cannot open file no=%d., Cannot find symbol %s., HSD_ArchiveParse error!, lbArchiveRelocate byte-order mismatch, [LbDvd] %s is not PRELOADed., Duplicate Free %08X, map collision error messages, MTHP diagnostics, and effect-loading table names.

Exposed asset and symbol-like names such as LbRb.dat, lbRumbleData, LbAd.dat, lbAudioLoadData, PdPm.dat, plLoadCommonData, PlCo.dat, ftLoadCommonData, EfMnData.dat, EfCoData.dat, EfGnData.dat, and many effKirby*DataTable names.
smart_moves: Used .asciz for recovered null-terminated strings instead of leaving them hidden inside .incbin blobs, making data searchable and usable for later decompilation.
Added .balign 4 after strings, matching the observed alignment pattern and maintaining layout around subsequent data.
Did not over-convert unknown bytes: residual non-string bytes remain as .incbin with adjusted baserom offsets and sizes.
Recovered assert strings that directly reveal original source expressions, enum names, field names, and macro names, which are especially valuable for naming future functions and structs.
Recovered original source filenames, giving strong subsystem boundaries and file-level provenance for later C translation work.
Recovered asset archive names and data-table names that can guide data-structure naming and resource-loading function identification.
decomp_lessons: When dumping strings from raw data sections, preserve binary layout by pairing each .asciz with the correct .balign and leaving unknown bytes as precise .incbin slices.
Assertion strings are high-confidence evidence for original variable, field, enum, and macro names; they should be mined before inventing names.
Source filename strings in .data/.sdata can identify the original compilation unit for nearby code and labels.

Repeated strings such as translate and !(jobj->flags & JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION) can indicate shared HSD/JObj assertion paths or reused animation helpers, but the PR slice does not prove exact function ownership.
Error and debug format strings often reveal subsystem prefixes such as [LbMem], [LbDvd], [LbMthp], and [EfASync], useful for grouping unknown functions.
Asset names like PlCo.dat, PdPm.dat, LbRb.dat, and Ef*.dat are practical anchors for naming loader functions and data tables.
A large string dump can be merged as assembly data work without changing C logic, but it substantially improves searchability and future matching.
naming_conventions: Recovered lowercase source filenames with subsystem prefixes: lbmemory.c, lbheap.c, lbfile.c, lbarchive.c, lbcardnew.c, camera.c, player.c, mpcoll.c, fighter.c.
Recovered library/system prefixes in messages and symbols: LbMem, LbDvd, LbMthp, EfASync, HSD_Archive, JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION.
Recovered camel/Pascal-style enum or constant names embedded in asserts: LbHeapStatus_Create, LbCardNewTaskArray_Max, CmSnapStatus_Sleep, Pl_ItemLog_Terminate, It_PKind_Terminate, Gm_Player_NumMax.

Recovered asset/data table naming patterns: LbRb.dat with lbRumbleData, LbAd.dat with lbAudioLoadData, PdPm.dat with plLoadCommonData, PlCo.dat with ftLoadCommonData, EfKb*.dat with effKirby*DataTable.
Recovered developer assert style often uses original expression text verbatim, including _p(name), range checks, and macro calls such as MTXDegToRad.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace large .incbin regions containing strings with a sequence of .asciz directives.
Insert .balign 4 after each recovered string to keep following labels and data aligned.
Adjust remaining .incbin baserom offsets and lengths around identified string spans rather than deleting unknown bytes.
Keep existing lbl_803B*/lbl_803C* labels when only the data contents are known and no better symbol name is proven.
Use recovered strings as anchors for later rodata/data references and for identifying nearby functions during matching.
Use source filename strings adjacent to assert strings to infer the likely original C file for functions referencing those labels.

review_feedback.author: camthesaxman
review_feedback.comment: Hold up on merging this. Dumping more strings I missed.
review_feedback.lesson: The author paused merging to complete the string dump, suggesting the PR was still being expanded after initial submission.

review_feedback.author: camthesaxman
review_feedback.comment: Ok, this is ready to merge.
review_feedback.lesson: No external review comments are present; readiness was self-declared after adding missed strings.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace lbl_803B*/lbl_803C* string labels with named rodata/data symbols.
Search for functions referencing lbmemory.c, lbheap.c, lbfile.c, and lbarchive.c strings to map library source files.
Search for assert string references containing JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION to identify animation or JObj transform functions.
Search for asset names PlCo.dat, PdPm.dat, LbRb.dat, LbAd.dat, and EfKb*.dat in later loader decompilation PRs.
Compare later data.s/sdata.s changes to ensure these .asciz/.balign conversions remained stable and were not corrected for alignment or string boundaries.
